topic: China overtime and working-time payroll controls for foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Establishes working-hour framework and rest-day/public-holiday overtime compensation principles.
Provides baseline for employer overtime payment obligations and working-time limits.

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Supports contract, labor-rights, and dispute-risk framework relevant to overtime and compensation handling.
Provides employer compliance baseline for evidence and lawful treatment in labor disputes.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Anchors contribution administration controls in payroll processing and post-run reconciliation.
Supports payroll governance model where compensation changes are mapped consistently to statutory processing.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Multinational employers need process-discipline and consistent documentation in China employment operations.
Dispute exposure is frequently linked to execution quality, not just policy awareness.

consensus: Overtime compliance risk for foreign employers is primarily an execution issue across attendance, approvals, and payroll conversion.
A documented, role-owned workflow with timeline gates reduces payroll rework and labor dispute risk.
Evidence quality and consistent communication are central to defensibility.
uncertainties: Detailed local practice on evidence expectations and overtime treatment can vary by city and case facts.
Some implementation details require local confirmation where rules or practice are interpreted differently.
English-language summaries may not capture all nuances of Chinese-language implementing guidance.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Build an attendance-to-payroll control tower with D-7 and D-3 gates, standardized exception handling, and auditable approval records.
